title: Rice Pulav
tagline: One-pot pulav with a fresh-ground coconut-garlic-chilli masala, bulked with soya so it actually hits protein.
macroNotes: Estimated from ingredients. To cut it toward ~600 kcal/serving, drop the oil to 1 tbsp and the rice to 3/4 cup.

items: 100 g dry soya chunks (50 g per serving)
Splash of water to help it blend
1 cup basmati rice (~185 g dry), rinsed until the water runs clear
2 cups mixed vegetables (~250 g) — potato, cauliflower, green beans, peas, whatever you like
2 cups hot water (2:1 water to rice)

steps.title: Prep the soya chunks
steps.body: Soak 100 g dry soya chunks in boiling water for 10 minutes until soft. Drain, then squeeze them dry hard — get all the water out or they'll taste bland and turn the pulav watery. Squeeze, refill with fresh water, squeeze again. Set aside.

steps.title: Rinse the rice
steps.body: Rinse 1 cup basmati (~185 g) until the water runs clear. Soak for 15 minutes, then drain. (Soaking is optional but gives longer, fluffier grains.)

steps.title: Chop the veg
steps.body: Cut 2 cups (~250 g) mixed vegetables — potato, cauliflower, beans, peas — into bite-sized pieces.

steps.title: Grind the masala
steps.body: Blend 3 tbsp dry coconut + 1 inch ginger + 6 cloves garlic + 2 green chillies + 1 inch cinnamon with a splash of water into a coarse paste.

steps.title: Bhuno the masala
steps.body: Heat 2 tbsp oil in a heavy pot over medium heat. Add the ground paste and fry 3–4 minutes, stirring, until the raw smell is gone and the oil starts to separate at the edges.

steps.title: Toast the soya
steps.body: Add the squeezed-dry soya chunks and fry 3–4 minutes until they pick up colour and start catching on the pot. This step is what makes them taste good instead of spongy — don't skip it.

steps.title: Toast the rice
steps.body: Add the drained rice and fry gently 2 minutes, stirring, until each grain is coated in oil and slightly translucent. Be gentle — you don't want broken grains.

steps.title: Add the ground spices
steps.body: Add 1/2 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp red chilli powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, 1/2 tsp garam masala, 1 tsp salt. Stir for 1 minute to bloom the spices.

steps.title: Add the vegetables
steps.body: Tip in the 2 cups chopped vegetables and stir for 2 minutes to coat.

steps.title: Add hot water and cook
steps.body: Pour in 2 cups hot water (hot, not cold — cold water shocks the rice and makes it cook unevenly). Stir once, bring to a boil, then cover and drop to the lowest heat. Cook 12–15 minutes until the water is absorbed and the rice is tender.

steps.title: Rest
steps.body: Turn off the heat and leave it covered for 5 minutes. Don't lift the lid. Then fluff with a fork.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Scatter 3 tbsp chopped fresh coriander over the top.

source: Original note in Hinglish, translated and measured out.
